observed: The prior workflow invoked mutable-directory generators rather than repository-native commit-bound discovery.

The updated workflow checks out the exact event commit with full history, verifies HEAD identity and a clean tree, runs both fail-closed continuity test suites, discovers queue records from HEAD through git object reads, rejects non-accepted output, verifies the result source_commit equals HEAD, and uploads only the accepted derived artifact.
inferred: Queue selection artifacts produced by this workflow cannot silently incorporate uncommitted files or queue records from another commit.
proposed: Require this workflow's accepted artifact before autonomous teams select work from the effective queue.
superseded: CI reconciliation based on mutable directory enumeration and older effective-queue generators.
unresolved: No GitHub Actions run was associated with the workflow commit at evidence-recording time.
Autonomous startup does not yet consume the accepted artifact.

static_contract_checks: full history available for git object discovery
effective-queue gate and discovery tests invoked
artifact upload occurs only after acceptance

privacy_boundary: Public repository paths, commit identities, abstract queue state, and generated operational evidence only; no private chat, health material, credentials, personal identifiers, or unreviewed uploads.
falsification_route: A workflow run that accepts a dirty checkout, emits an artifact with a different source_commit, includes an uncommitted queue update, omits a committed queue update, or uploads rejected output falsifies the implementation claim.
